### Post by Cereal2nd on 2005-09-26
hi,

i'm trying to setup a dual screen setup.

i have 2 eizo l557 screens and a geforece fx 5200 card.

this is my current xorg.conf
```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following commands:
#
#   cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.conf.custom
#   sudo sh -c 'md5sum /etc/X11/xorg.conf >/var/lib/xfree86/xorg.conf.md5sum'
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
    FontPath    "unix/:7100"            # local font server
    # if the local font server has problems, we can fall back on these
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/CID"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
    FontPath    "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
        # paths to defoma fonts
    FontPath    "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
    FontPath    "/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/CID"
EndSection

Section "Module"
    Load    "GLcore"
    Load    "i2c"
    Load    "bitmap"
    Load    "ddc"
    Load    "dri"
    Load    "extmod"
    Load    "freetype"
    Load    "glx"
    Load    "int10"
    Load    "type1"
    Load    "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier    "Generic Keyboard"
    Driver        "kbd"
    Option        "CoreKeyboard"
    Option        "XkbRules"    "xorg"
    Option        "XkbModel"    "pc105"
    Option        "XkbLayout"    "be"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
    Identifier    "Configured Mouse"
    Driver        "mouse"
    Option        "CorePointer"
    Option        "Device"        "/dev/input/mice"
    Option        "Protocol"        "ImPS/2"
    Option        "Emulate3Buttons"    "true"
    Option        "ZAxisMapping"        "4 5"
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier    "NVIDIA Corporation NV34 [GeForce FX 5200] 1"
    Driver    "nv"
    BusID        "PCI:1:0:0"
    Screen 0
EndSection

Section "Device"
    Identifier    "NVIDIA Corporation NV34 [GeForce FX 5200] 2"
    Driver    "nv"
    BusID        "PCI:1:0:0"
    Screen 1
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
    Identifier    "L557 1"
    Option        "DPMS"
EndSection

section "Monitor"
    Identifier    "L557 2"
    Option        "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Screen"
    Identifier    "Screen right"
    Device        "NVIDIA Corporation NV34 [GeForce FX 5200] 2"
    Monitor        "L557 2"
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        1
        Modes        "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        4
        Modes        "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        8
        Modes        "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        15
        Modes        "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        16
        Modes        "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        24
        Modes        "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
EndSection        

Section "Screen"
    Identifier    "Screen left"
    Device        "NVIDIA Corporation NV34 [GeForce FX 5200] 1"
    Monitor        "L557 1"
    DefaultDepth    24
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        1
        Modes        "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        4
        Modes        "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        8
        Modes        "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        15
        Modes        "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        16
        Modes        "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
    SubSection "Display"
        Depth        24
        Modes        "1280x1024" "1024x768" "800x600" "720x400" "640x480"
    EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
    Identifier    "Default Layout"
    Screen    "Screen left" 0 0
    Screen    "Screen right" RightOf "Screen left"
    InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
    InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
EndSection

Section "DRI"
    Mode    0666
EndSection

``` 
and i get the folowing error when starting x

> 
requested entity already in use
 
any ideas on this problem?

### Post by Brayn on 2005-09-26
I got dual screens working on my ati radeon 9200se.

heres my xorg.conf ```
# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following commands:
#
#   cp /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.conf.custom
#   sudo sh -c 'md5sum /etc/X11/xorg.conf >/var/lib/xfree86/xorg.conf.md5sum'
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg

Section "Files"
	FontPath	"unix/:7100"			# local font server
	# if the local font server has problems, we can fall back on these
	FontPath	"/usr/lib/X11/fonts/misc"
	FontPath	"/usr/lib/X11/fonts/cyrillic"
	FontPath	"/usr/lib/X11/fonts/100dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/lib/X11/fonts/75dpi/:unscaled"
	FontPath	"/usr/lib/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath	"/usr/lib/X11/fonts/CID"
	FontPath	"/usr/lib/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath	"/usr/lib/X11/fonts/75dpi"
        # paths to defoma fonts
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/TrueType"
	FontPath	"/var/lib/defoma/x-ttcidfont-conf.d/dirs/CID"
EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load	"bitmap"
	Load	"dbe"
	Load	"ddc"
	Load	"dri"
	Load	"extmod"
	Load	"freetype"
	Load	"glx"
	Load	"int10"
	Load	"record"
	Load	"type1"
	Load	"vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"keyboard"
	Option		"CoreKeyboard"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc104"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"us"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/input/mice"
	Option		"Protocol"		"ImPS/2"
	Option		"Emulate3Buttons"	"true"
	Option		"ZAxisMapping"		"4 5"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "Monitor0"
	VendorName   "Monitor Vendor"
	ModelName    "Monitor 1600x1200"
	DisplaySize  370	280
	HorizSync    31.5 - 94.0
	VertRefresh  50.0 - 90.0
	Option	    "dpms"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "Monitor1"
	VendorName   "Monitor Vendor"
	ModelName    "Monitor 1280x1024"
	HorizSync    31.5 - 79.0
	VertRefresh  50.0 - 90.0
	Option	    "dpms"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "Videocard0"
	Driver      "radeon"
	VendorName  "Videocard vendor"
	BoardName   "ATI Radeon 9200SE"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "Videocard1"
	Driver      "radeon"
	VendorName  "Videocard Vendor"
	BoardName   "ATI Radeon 9200SE"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
	Screen      1
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Screen0"
	Device     "Videocard0"
	Monitor    "Monitor0"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     16
		Modes    "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1600x1200" "1400x1050" "1280x960" "1280x800" "1280x1024" "1152x864" "1152x768" "1024x768" "800x600" "640x480"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier "Screen1"
	Device     "Videocard1"
	Monitor    "Monitor1"
	DefaultDepth     24
	SubSection "Display"
		Viewport   0 0
		Depth     24
		Modes    "1600x1200"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "DRI"
	Group        0
	Mode         0666
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Multihead layout"
	Screen      0  "Screen0" LeftOf "Screen1"
	Screen      1  "Screen1" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse" "CorePointer"
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard" "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "Xinerama" "on"
	Option	    "Clone" "off"
EndSection
``` 

hope it helps.

### Post by xterminus on 2005-09-26
[QUOTE=Cereal2nd]hi,
i'm trying to setup a dual screen setup.
I have 2 eizo l557 screens and a geforece fx 5200 card.
this is my current xorg.conf
and i get the folowing error when starting x
any ideas on this problem?[/QUOTE]


Your defining the same PCI bus over and over.  You either need to identify the second video card, or identify the output on for each device.

On nvidia cards, I think this looks something like...

```
 BusID "PCI:1:0:0"
Screen 0
```

and then for the second output

```
 BusID "PCI:1:0:0"
Screen 1
```

---

```
 BusID "PCI:1:0:0"
Screen 0
``` 
and then for the second output

```
 BusID "PCI:1:0:0"
Screen 1
```[/quote]

i'm doing it not?
```

 Section "Device"
     Identifier    "NVIDIA Corporation NV34 [GeForce FX 5200] 1"
     Driver    "nv"
     BusID        "PCI:1:0:0"
     Screen 0
 EndSection
 
 Section "Device"
     Identifier    "NVIDIA Corporation NV34 [GeForce FX 5200] 2"
     Driver    "nv"
     BusID        "PCI:1:0:0"
     Screen 1
 EndSection

```

### Post by FLeiXiuS on 2005-09-26
```
Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Multihead layout"
	Screen      0  "Screen0" LeftOf "Screen1"
	Screen      1  "Screen1" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse" "CorePointer"
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard" "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "Xinerama" "on"
	Option	    "Clone" "off"
EndSection

```
In this section it's safe to remove the trailing [ [COLOR="DarkOrange"]0 0[/COLOR] ] after [ [COLOR="DarkOrange"]Screen      1  "Screen1" 0 0[/COLOR] ].

After that, save and restart GDM.  Please give me insights on errors messages.  I may be completely wrong ;-), but this has been a known problem for me right now.

### Post by xterminus on 2005-09-26
[QUOTE=Cereal2nd]i'm doing it not?
```

 Section "Device"
     Identifier    "NVIDIA Corporation NV34 [GeForce FX 5200] 1"
     Driver    "nv"
     BusID        "PCI:1:0:0"
     Screen 0
 EndSection
 
 Section "Device"
     Identifier    "NVIDIA Corporation NV34 [GeForce FX 5200] 2"

     BusID        "PCI:1:0:0"

 EndSection

That looks right actually.  I missed that (my bad).  The rest of your config file looks okay with me except for one thing.  I can't get X too boot up if I don't specify the HorizSync and VertRefresh for each monitor.  You have yours blank.

Other than that, maybe if you could paste or attach the output of /var/log/Xorg.0.log

### Post by antariksh on 2005-09-27
In the **"Device"** section of your /etc/X11/xorg.conf, the following line is **incorrect**:

> Driver    "nv"

"nv" is the open-source nvidia driver that does not support dual-head. You need the official nvidia driver("**nvidia**" instead of "**nv**"). Start by installing **nvidia-glx** package with apt-get or synaptic, and follow it up by opening a terminal and running the command:

> sudo nvidia-glx-config enable

Now replace the **Driver "nv"** in your xorg.conf with **Driver "nvidia"** and see if it works by logging out and restarting your X-server (CTRL+ALT+backspace at at login screen). If it does, stop reading right here and go enjoy your dual head!

...

Didn't work? OK, you may want to replace your **Xinerama** method of dual display with **TwinView** which is an nvidia-specific method.

Here are relevant sections of my /etc/X11/xorg.conf that may help you. I use TwinView instead of Xinerama(the former is a different and nvidia-specific method).

- the **"Module"** Section must **disable** **"dri"** and **"GLCore"**(add a **#** at the beginning of the corresponding lines) as follows:

> 
...
# Load   "dri"
...
# Load   "GLCore"
...



- Section **"Device"** must be as follows:

> 
Section "Device"
    Identifier "NVIDIA Corporation NV17 [GeForce4 MX 460]"
    Driver  **"nvidia"**
    BusID  "PCI:1:0:0" 
    Option   **"TwinView"** "True"
    Option   "MetaModes" "1280x1024, 1024x768; 1152x864, 1024x768; 1024x768, 800x600"
    Option   "SecondMonitorHorizSync" "30-69"
    Option   "SecondMonitorVertRefresh" "50-120"
    Option   **"TwinViewOrientation"** "RightOf"
EndSection

Obviously you will need adapt the settings to your configuration and desired setup. However I have highlighted important lines. Remember that with **TwinView**, you **only need one "Device" section**.

Note the configurations for the following sections and compare them with the corresponding sections in your xorg.conf. Once again, you may need to adapt the actual settings to your specific setup and the resolutions you wish to run. Also note that you **only need one screen section** with TwinView.

> 
Section "Screen"
 Identifier **"Default Screen"**
 Device  "NVIDIA Corporation NV17 [GeForce4 MX 460]"
 Monitor  "Generic Monitor"

 [Display resolutions etc...]

EndSection

> 
Section "ServerLayout"
   Identifier "Default Layout"
   Screen  **"Default Screen"**
   InputDevice "Generic Keyboard"
   InputDevice "Configured Mouse"
EndSection

---
